      Damaged DNA binding is a well-supported extra-ribosomal molecular function
      of human RPS3 (multiple independent IDA assays: PMID:14706345,
      PMID:18610840, PMID:16737853). It is a genuine moonlighting activity but
      secondary to the core ribosomal structural function, so retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:14706345
      supporting_text: >-
        An even more dramatic five orders of magnitude higher binding affinity
        for AP DNA was found for hS3 as opposed to hOGG1.
- term:
    id: GO:0006284
    label: base-excision repair
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:15518571
  review:
    summary: >-
      Human RPS3 physically interacts with the BER enzymes OGG1 and APE1/Ref-1
      and can stimulate OGG1 N-glycosylase activity, supporting participation in
      base-excision repair as a binding partner/modulator rather than as a core
      BER enzyme.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      RPS3 participates in BER through cofactor-like interactions (binding and
      stimulating OGG1/APE1; PMID:18973764 shows it also stimulates UNG), but
      this is an extra-ribosomal moonlighting role; note that under some
      conditions bound RPS3 can inhibit OGG1 repair (PMID:16737853), so the role
      is context-dependent. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:15518571
      supporting_text: >-
        hOGG1 N-glycosylase activity becomes increasingly more robust in the
        presence of hS3. Human S3 was found to co-immunoprecipitate with both
        hOGG1 and APE/Ref-1
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:19059439
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is phosphorylated by PKCdelta, which interacts with RPS3 and
      mobilizes non-ribosomal RPS3 to the nucleus for DNA repair.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding term; the specific PKCdelta interaction is part of
      the regulated extra-ribosomal DNA-repair switch and is non-core relative
      to the ribosomal function. Retained as non-core documentation.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19059439
      supporting_text: >-
        PKCdelta phosphorylates rpS3 resulting in its mobilization in the
        nucleus to repair damaged DNA
- term:
    id: GO:0006281
    label: DNA repair
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:19059439
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 knockdown increased sensitivity to genotoxic stress, and the
      phosphorylation-dependent repair endonuclease activity of non-ribosomal
      RPS3 was required for this protection, supporting an extra-ribosomal DNA
      repair role.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      DNA repair is a documented extra-ribosomal moonlighting function but is
      secondary to the core ribosomal role. Note that other work reports RPS3
      knockdown protects cells from genotoxic stress (PMID:17049931), so the net
      effect on repair is context-dependent.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19059439
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 knock-down cells showed more sensitivity to genotoxic stress than
        control cells, and this sensitivity was corrected by overexpressed
        wild-type rpS3 but not by phosphorylation defective rpS3
- term:
    id: GO:0007059
    label: chromosome segregation
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:23131551
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 depletion caused mitotic arrest at metaphase with abnormal spindle
      shape and chromosome movement, consistent with a role in proper
      chromosome segregation via its mitotic-spindle/microtubule function.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      The chromosome segregation phenotype is downstream of RPS3's reported
      microtubule-associated/spindle role during mitosis, an extra-ribosomal
      moonlighting context rather than the core ribosomal function. Retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23131551
      supporting_text: >-
        Depletion of the rpS3 proteins caused mitotic arrest during the
        metaphase. Furthermore, the shape of the spindle and chromosome movement
        in the rpS3 depleted cell was abnormal.
- term:
    id: GO:0010628
    label: positive regulation of gene expression
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:20605787
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 up-regulated the proapoptotic proteins Dp5/Hrk and Bim by binding to
      and acting synergistically with the transcription factor E2F1 in neuronal
      apoptosis.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This positive regulation of gene expression is an extra-ribosomal
      transcriptional-coregulator activity (via E2F1), context-specific to
      neuronal apoptosis, and non-core relative to the ribosomal role. The
      mechanistic E2F1 interaction is captured by the DNA-binding transcription
      factor binding annotation from the same reference.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20605787
      supporting_text: >-
        RPS3 induced neuronal apoptosis, up-regulating proapoptotic proteins
        Dp5/Hrk and Bim by binding to E2F1 and acting synergistically with it.
- term:
    id: GO:0005743
    label: mitochondrial inner membrane
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      Under elevated ROS, RPS3 accumulates in the mitochondria (entry mediated
      by HSP90/HSP70/TOM70) to participate in mtDNA damage repair.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This IDA captures stress-induced (ROS-triggered) mitochondrial
      accumulation of RPS3 for mtDNA damage surveillance, a regulated
      extra-ribosomal context, not constitutive inner-membrane residency. It is
      kept as non-core, distinct from the broad IEA mitochondrial inner membrane
      annotation that was removed.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        Increased ROS induces rpS3 accumulation in the mitochondria for DNA
        repair while significantly decreasing the cellular protein synthesis.
- term:
    id: GO:0005759
    label: mitochondrial matrix
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 accumulates within mitochondria under oxidative stress to repair
      mitochondrial DNA, consistent with localization to the mitochondrial
      matrix where the mtDNA resides.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Stress-induced mitochondrial localization for mtDNA repair is a regulated
      extra-ribosomal moonlighting role, secondary to the core cytosolic
      ribosomal function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 accumulates in the mitochondria to repair damaged DNA due to the
        decreased interaction between rpS3 and HSP90 in the cytosol.
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:22510408
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 physically interacts with TRADD and is recruited to the
      death-inducing signaling complex (DISC) to promote caspase-dependent JNK
      activation and apoptosis under stress.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding term; the specific TRADD interaction underlies an
      extra-ribosomal pro-apoptotic moonlighting role and is non-core relative to
      the ribosomal function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:22510408
      supporting_text: >-
        This apoptotic effect was shown to result from the physical interaction
        between rpS3 and TRADD, as assessed by coimmunoprecipitation.
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:20041225
  review:
    summary: >-
      The enterohemorrhagic E. coli type III secretion effectors NleH1/NleH2
      bind the N-terminus of human RPS3 to subvert RPS3-dependent NF-kB function.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding term capturing a host-pathogen interaction
      relevant to RPS3's NF-kB role; non-core relative to the ribosomal
      function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20041225
      supporting_text: >-
        the E. coli O157:H7 nleH1 and nleH2 genes encode T3SS effectors that
        bind to the human ribosomal protein S3 (RPS3), a subunit of ... NF-kappaB
        transcriptional complexes
- term:
    id: GO:0140297
    label: DNA-binding transcription factor binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:20605787
  review:
    summary: >-
      Well-supported annotation. PMID:20605787 demonstrates that RPS3 binds to the E2F1
      transcription factor and acts synergistically with E2F1 to up-regulate proapoptotic
      proteins Dp5/Hrk and Bim. This interaction is regulated by Akt-mediated phosphorylation
      of RPS3, which perturbs its interaction with E2F1. This annotation is appropriate and
      supports the role of RPS3 as a transcriptional coregulator that binds transcription
      factors rather than DNA directly.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      The annotation to DNA-binding transcription factor binding is correct based on the
      evidence in PMID:20605787 showing RPS3 physically interacts with E2F1, a DNA-binding
      transcription factor. This is consistent with RPS3's role as a transcriptional
      coregulator that modulates transcription through protein-protein interactions with
      transcription factors (both E2F1 and NF-kappaB/RELA).
    supported_by:
      - reference_id: PMID:20605787
        supporting_text: >-
          RPS3 induced neuronal apoptosis, up-regulating proapoptotic proteins Dp5/Hrk and Bim
          by binding to E2F1 and acting synergistically with it. Akt-dependent phosphorylation
          of RPS3 inhibited its proapoptotic function and perturbed its interaction with E2F1.
- term:
    id: GO:0002181
    label: cytoplasmic translation
  evidence_type: IC
  original_reference_id: PMID:23636399
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is part of the human 80S ribosome resolved by cryo-EM, supporting its
      role in cytoplasmic translation.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytoplasmic translation is the core biological process for RPS3, inferred
      from its experimentally confirmed structural presence in the human
      cytosolic ribosome.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23636399
      supporting_text: >-
        Protein synthesis in all cells is carried out by macromolecular machines
        called ribosomes.
- term:
    id: GO:0003735
    label: structural constituent of ribosome
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23636399
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3/uS3 is resolved as a structural protein of the human cytosolic small
      ribosomal subunit in the 80S ribosome cryo-EM structure.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      This is the core, directly-evidenced molecular function of RPS3.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23636399
      supporting_text: >-
        Here we present structures of Drosophila melanogaster and Homo sapiens
        80S ribosomes in complex with the translation factor eEF2, E-site
        transfer RNA and Stm1-like proteins, based on high-resolution
        cryo-electron-microscopy density maps.
- term:
    id: GO:0030544
    label: Hsp70 protein binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 interacts with HSP70 (with HSP90 and TOM70) to regulate its entry
      into mitochondria during oxidative stress.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This is a specific, directly-evidenced chaperone interaction supporting
      RPS3's stress-induced mitochondrial trafficking, an extra-ribosomal
      moonlighting context. More informative than generic protein binding, but
      non-core relative to the ribosomal function.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        the accumulation of rpS3 was regulated by interaction with HSP90, HSP70,
        and TOM70.
- term:
    id: GO:0034614
    label: cellular response to reactive oxygen species
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      Elevated cellular ROS triggers RPS3 accumulation in mitochondria for mtDNA
      repair and a decrease in protein synthesis, indicating a cellular response
      to reactive oxygen species.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      RPS3's ROS-responsive mitochondrial relocalization is a regulated
      extra-ribosomal stress response, non-core relative to its ribosomal
      function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        When cellular ROS levels increase, the mitochondrial genes are highly
        vulnerable to DNA damage. Increased ROS induces rpS3 accumulation in the
        mitochondria for DNA repair
- term:
    id: GO:0019899
    label: enzyme binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:18973764
  review:
    summary: >-
      Human RPS3 interacts with uracil-DNA glycosylase (UNG) and stimulates its
      glycosylase activity, as shown by co-IP and GST pull-down assays.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Enzyme binding here captures a specific, directly-evidenced interaction
      with a BER enzyme (UNG); more informative than generic protein binding but
      part of RPS3's extra-ribosomal BER-cofactor moonlighting role, so non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:18973764
      supporting_text: >-
        We identified, for the first time, the interaction between hRpS3 and
        human uracil-DNA glycosylase (hUNG) ... hRpS3 dramatically stimulated the
        uracil-excision activity of hUNG, whereas hRpS3 alone had no cleavage
        activity.
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:28065601
  review:
    summary: >-
      Study of ZNF598-mediated site-specific 40S ribosomal ubiquitination
      initiating ribosome-associated quality control; RPS3/uS3 is a 40S
      component in this context.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding term arising from a ribosome-associated quality
      control study; for an abundant 40S protein this association is expected and
      uninformative as a specific molecular function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:28065601
      supporting_text: >-
        Initiation of Quality Control during Poly(A) Translation Requires
        Site-Specific Ribosome Ubiquitination.
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:28132843
  review:
    summary: >-
      ZNF598 and RACK1 regulate 40S ribosomal ubiquitylation during
      ribosome-associated quality control; RACK1 regulates ubiquitination of
      RPS2, RPS3, and RPS20.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding term from a ribosome-associated quality control
      study. The specific finding (RACK1-regulated RPS3 ubiquitylation) is part
      of ribosome regulation rather than a discrete RPS3 binding function;
      retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:28132843
      supporting_text: >-
        RACK1 regulates RPS2, RPS3, and RPS20 ubiquitylation
- term:
    id: GO:0005634
    label: nucleus
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:17560175
  review:
    summary: >-
      Following genotoxic stress, human RPS3 translocates from the cytoplasm to
      the nucleus (dependent on ERK-mediated T42 phosphorylation), where it
      co-localizes with 8-oxoG damage foci.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Stimulus-dependent nuclear translocation is well documented but represents
      a regulated extra-ribosomal DNA-damage-response context rather than the
      core cytosolic ribosomal location. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:17560175
      supporting_text: >-
        hS3 is capable of carrying out both roles by its ex vivo translocation
        from the cytoplasm to the nucleus as a consequence of genotoxic stress.
- term:
    id: GO:0005634
    label: nucleus
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:21399639
  review:
    summary: >-
      Nuclear localization of RPS3 in response to activating stimuli requires
      IKKβ-mediated phosphorylation of RPS3 Ser209, as shown using
      phospho-mutant analysis.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Regulated, stimulus-dependent nuclear localization tied to NF-kB
      signaling; non-core relative to the cytosolic ribosomal location. Retained
      as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:21399639
      supporting_text: >-
        phosphorylation of RPS3 Ser209 by the kinase IKKβ was crucial for
        nuclear localization of RPS3 in response to activating stimuli
- term:
    id: GO:0005925
    label: focal adhesion
  evidence_type: HDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:21423176
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 was detected in a high-throughput proteomic analysis of isolated
      focal adhesions.
    action: MARK_AS_OVER_ANNOTATED
    reason: >-
      Detection of an abundant ribosomal protein in a focal-adhesion proteome is
      most plausibly a co-isolation/contaminant signal rather than evidence of an
      RPS3-specific focal adhesion role. No targeted validation supports stable
      focal-adhesion localization, so this is treated as an over-annotation.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:21423176
      supporting_text: >-
        we performed proteomic analysis of isolated focal adhesions ... We
        identified 905 focal adhesion proteins
- term:
    id: GO:0071159
    label: NF-kappaB complex
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18045535
  review:
    summary: >-
      Well-supported annotation. PMID:18045535 used tandem affinity purification and mass
      spectrometry to identify RPS3 as a component of the NF-kappaB complex. The study clearly
      shows that RPS3 is "a non-Rel subunit of p65 homodimer and p65-p50 heterodimer DNA-binding
      complexes." RPS3 interacts with RELA (p65) and forms part of the NF-kappaB transcription
      factor complex bound to chromatin in response to activating stimuli.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      The proteomic approach in PMID:18045535 directly demonstrates RPS3 is a component of
      NF-kappaB complexes. This was validated by co-IP experiments and functional studies showing
      RPS3 is essential for NF-kappaB function in gene regulation.
    supported_by:
      - reference_id: PMID:18045535
        supporting_text: >-
          We identified ribosomal protein S3 (RPS3), a KH domain protein, as a non-Rel subunit
          of p65 homodimer and p65-p50 heterodimer DNA-binding complexes that synergistically
          enhances DNA binding.
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18045535
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is present in the cytosol, where it associates with NF-kB inhibitory
      complexes prior to stimulus-induced nuclear translocation.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 (cytosolic
      ribosome), consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:18045535
      supporting_text: >-
        lymphocyte-activating stimuli caused nuclear translocation of RPS3,
        parallel to p65
- term:
    id: GO:1905053
    label: positive regulation of base-excision repair
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18973764
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 stimulates the uracil-excision (glycosylase) activity of UNG and its
      knockdown reduces uracil-excision activity in cell extracts, supporting
      positive regulation of base-excision repair.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      RPS3 positively regulates BER as a cofactor that stimulates UNG (and OGG1,
      PMID:15518571) rather than acting as a core BER enzyme. This is a genuine
      extra-ribosomal moonlighting role, retained as non-core. Note that under
      conditions where RPS3 binds 8-oxoG it can instead inhibit OGG1 repair
      (PMID:16737853), so the regulation is context-dependent.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:18973764
      supporting_text: >-
        The disruption of hRpS3 by small-interfering RNA (siRNA-hRpS3)
        transfection reduced the uracil-excision activity preserved in cell
        extracts, whereas the supplement of purified hRpS3 retained
        uracil-excision activity.
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:24965446
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 was identified among ribosomal proteins/ribonucleoproteins that
      co-purify with the pestivirus N-terminal protease Npro in the
      ribonucleoprotein complex.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding from a viral-protein interaction proteomics study;
      for an abundant ribosomal protein this co-purification is expected and
      uninformative as a specific molecular function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:24965446
      supporting_text: >-
        Host factors that interact with the pestivirus N-terminal protease,
        Npro, are components of the ribonucleoprotein complex
- term:
    id: GO:0017148
    label: negative regulation of translation
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20217897
  review:
    summary: >-
      Free (non-ribosomal) RPS3 binds its own cognate mRNA via its C-terminal
      domain and represses its own translation in a feedback mechanism.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This is a specific, directly-evidenced autoregulatory negative regulation
      of translation. It is a genuine but specialized function distinct from the
      core structural ribosomal role, so retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20217897
      supporting_text: >-
        We here report an additional function of rpS3 in which it represses its
        own translation by binding to its cognate mRNA.
- term:
    id: GO:0061481
    label: response to TNF agonist
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20041225
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 participates in NF-kB-dependent transcriptional responses; in this
      study its nuclear abundance and RPS3/NF-kB-dependent reporter activity are
      modulated, and RPS3 functions within TNF/NF-kB activation pathways.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This term reflects RPS3's extra-ribosomal participation in NF-kB-mediated
      cytokine responses, a context-dependent moonlighting role rather than the
      core ribosomal function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20041225
      supporting_text: >-
        NleH1 repressed the transcription of a RPS3/NF-kappaB-dependent reporter
        plasmid
- term:
    id: GO:0005634
    label: nucleus
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20041225
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 has a nuclear pool whose abundance is modulated during NF-kB
      signaling (NleH1 reduced the nuclear abundance of RPS3).
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Nuclear localization in the NF-kB context is a regulated extra-ribosomal
      pool, non-core relative to the cytosolic ribosomal location. Retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20041225
      supporting_text: >-
        NleH1, but not NleH2, reduced the nuclear abundance of RPS3 without
        altering the p50 or p65 NF-kappaB subunits
- term:
    id: GO:1901224
    label: positive regulation of non-canonical NF-kappaB signal transduction
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20041225
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 acts as a specifier subunit that positively contributes to
      NF-kB-dependent transcription; bacterial effector binding to RPS3 subverts
      this NF-kB function.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      RPS3's positive contribution to NF-kB-dependent transcription is a
      well-supported extra-ribosomal moonlighting role. The specific
      "non-canonical" qualifier is not strongly distinguished in this reference
      (which centers on RPS3/p65 transcriptional complexes), but the broad
      positive NF-kB regulation is supported; retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20041225
      supporting_text: >-
        Bacterial effector binding to ribosomal protein s3 subverts NF-kappaB
        function.
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3's mitochondrial entry under oxidative stress is regulated by
      interaction with HSP90, HSP70, and TOM70.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding term from the mitochondrial mtDNA-surveillance
      study; the specific chaperone/TOM70 interactions are captured more
      informatively by the Hsp70/Hsp90 binding annotations from the same
      reference. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        the accumulation of rpS3 was regulated by interaction with HSP90, HSP70,
        and TOM70.
- term:
    id: GO:0045739
    label: positive regulation of DNA repair
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      Increasing mitochondrial RPS3 levels reduced cellular ROS and rescued
      mtDNA damage, indicating RPS3 positively contributes to repair of damaged
      mitochondrial DNA under oxidative stress.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Positive regulation of (mitochondrial) DNA repair is a stress-induced
      extra-ribosomal moonlighting role, non-core relative to the ribosomal
      function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        cellular ROS was decreased and mtDNA damage was rescued when levels of
        rpS3 were increased in the mitochondria.
- term:
    id: GO:0051879
    label: Hsp90 protein binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 interacts with HSP90 in the cytosol; geldanamycin disruption of this
      interaction stimulates RPS3 accumulation in mitochondria.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Specific, directly-evidenced HSP90 interaction governing RPS3's
      stress-induced mitochondrial trafficking; more informative than generic
      protein binding but part of an extra-ribosomal context, so non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        Pretreatment with geldanamycin, which binds to the ATP pocket of HSP90,
        significantly decreased the interaction of rpS3 with HSP90 and stimulated
        the accumulation of rpS3 in the mitochondria.
- term:
    id: GO:0070301
    label: cellular response to hydrogen peroxide
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23911537
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 relocalizes to mitochondria in response to oxidative (ROS/H2O2)
      stress to participate in mtDNA damage surveillance.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Specific oxidative-stress (hydrogen peroxide) response captured by RPS3's
      stress-induced mitochondrial trafficking, an extra-ribosomal role; non-core
      relative to the ribosomal function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23911537
      supporting_text: >-
        Increased ROS induces rpS3 accumulation in the mitochondria for DNA
        repair while significantly decreasing the cellular protein synthesis.
- term:
    id: GO:0005886
    label: plasma membrane
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:22510408
  review:
    summary: >-
      During stress-induced apoptosis, GFP-RPS3 co-localized with TRADD around
      the plasma membrane (and in the cytoplasm) at the death-inducing signaling
      complex.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Plasma-membrane-proximal localization is a transient, apoptosis-context
      observation tied to DISC recruitment, not a stable RPS3 plasma membrane
      residency. Retained as non-core given the specialized, context-dependent
      nature.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:22510408
      supporting_text: >-
        GFP-rpS3 colocalized with TRADD around the plasma membrane and in the
        cytoplasm during apoptosis.
- term:
    id: GO:0008017
    label: microtubule binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23131551
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 localizes on the mitotic spindle and functions as a
      microtubule-associated protein, with its depletion decreasing microtubule
      polymerization.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Microtubule binding supports a directly-evidenced mitotic
      microtubule-associated-protein moonlighting role, distinct from and
      secondary to the core ribosomal function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23131551
      supporting_text: >-
        we concluded that rpS3 acts as a microtubule associated protein (MAP)
        and regulates spindle dynamics during mitosis.
- term:
    id: GO:0015631
    label: tubulin binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23131551
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 associates with the mitotic spindle and microtubules as a
      microtubule-associated protein.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Tubulin/microtubule binding supports RPS3's directly-evidenced mitotic
      MAP role, an extra-ribosomal moonlighting function secondary to the core
      ribosomal role. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23131551
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 specifically localizes on the mitotic spindle ... rpS3 acts as a
        microtubule associated protein (MAP)
- term:
    id: GO:0031116
    label: positive regulation of microtubule polymerization
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:23131551
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 depletion decreased microtubule polymerization, indicating RPS3
      promotes microtubule polymerization during mitosis.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Directly supported by loss-of-function evidence, but part of RPS3's mitotic
      microtubule moonlighting role rather than the core ribosomal function.
      Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23131551
      supporting_text: >-
        Microtubule (MT) polymerization also decreased in rpS3 depleted cells,
        suggesting that rpS3 is involved in spindle dynamics.
- term:
    id: GO:0051225
    label: spindle assembly
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:23131551
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 depletion produced abnormal spindle shape and mitotic arrest,
      consistent with a role in proper spindle assembly.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Spindle assembly is supported by loss-of-function data but reflects RPS3's
      mitotic microtubule moonlighting role, secondary to the core ribosomal
      function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23131551
      supporting_text: >-
        the shape of the spindle and chromosome movement in the rpS3 depleted
        cell was abnormal.
- term:
    id: GO:0072686
    label: mitotic spindle
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:23131551
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 specifically localizes on the mitotic spindle during mitosis as shown
      by direct imaging.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Direct imaging supports mitotic spindle localization, an extra-ribosomal
      moonlighting context distinct from and secondary to the cytosolic
      ribosomal location. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:23131551
      supporting_text: >-
        When we examined localization of ribosomal proteins in mitosis, we found
        that rpS3 specifically localizes on the mitotic spindle.
- term:
    id: GO:1902231
    label: positive regulation of intrinsic apoptotic signaling pathway in response
      to DNA damage
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:22510408
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 sensitizes genotoxic (DNA-damage) stress-induced apoptosis by
      activating JNK in a caspase-dependent manner through interaction with
      TRADD at the DISC.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      RPS3's pro-apoptotic activity in response to genotoxic stress is an
      extra-ribosomal moonlighting role demonstrated by overexpression/interaction
      experiments, secondary to the core ribosomal function. Retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:22510408
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 sensitizes genotoxic stress-induced apoptosis by activating JNK
        through a caspase dependent manner.
- term:
    id: GO:0003723
    label: RNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20217897
  review:
    summary: >-
      Free RPS3 binds RNA, including its own cognate mRNA and 18S rRNA, via its
      C-terminal domain (independent of the KH domain).
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      RNA binding is a directly-evidenced activity consistent with RPS3's KH
      domain and its rRNA contacts in the ribosome, and underlies its
      translational autoregulation. It is secondary to the core structural
      ribosomal role; retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20217897
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 protein was found to interact with various RNAs-endogenous rpS3,
        18S rRNA. The S3-C terminal domain was shown to be the major mRNA
        binding domain of rpS3
- term:
    id: GO:0004520
    label: DNA endonuclease activity
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20605787
  review:
    summary: >-
      Nuclear-accumulated RPS3 is reported to function as an endonuclease,
      contributing to DNA repair activity in neuronal cells.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      DNA endonuclease activity reflects RPS3's historically reported
      AP/base-damage endonuclease moonlighting activity. The intrinsic catalytic
      status of human RPS3 remains debated (PMID:18610840 finds only AP-site
      beta-elimination and questions in vivo relevance), so this is retained as a
      non-core, context-dependent activity rather than a core function.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20605787
      supporting_text: >-
        nuclear translocation and accumulation of RPS3, where it functions as an
        endonuclease.
- term:
    id: GO:0005634
    label: nucleus
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:21871177
  review:
    summary: >-
      Cdk1-mediated phosphorylation of RPS3 at Thr221 during G2/M promotes its
      nuclear accumulation.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Regulated, cell-cycle-dependent nuclear accumulation is an extra-ribosomal
      context, non-core relative to the cytosolic ribosomal location. Retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:21871177
      supporting_text: >-
        the phosphorylation of rpS3 by Cdk1 occurs at Thr221 during G2/M phase
        and, moreover, that this event is important for nuclear accumulation of
        rpS3.
- term:
    id: GO:0005737
    label: cytoplasm
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20217897
  review:
    summary: >-
      A pool of free RPS3 exists in the cytoplasm (outside ribosomes), where it
      binds its own mRNA to autoregulate translation.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytoplasm is the expected, core location for RPS3 (cytosolic ribosome and
      free cytoplasmic pool), consistent with accepted cytosol/cytoplasm
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20217897
      supporting_text: >-
        Most of the rpS3 pool is located in the ribosome while the minority
        exists in free form in the cytoplasm.
- term:
    id: GO:0005737
    label: cytoplasm
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:21871177
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is present in the cytoplasm, with a phosphorylation-regulated shift to
      nuclear accumulation during G2/M.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytoplasm is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3, consistent with
      its cytosolic ribosomal function.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:21871177
      supporting_text: >-
        Decreased phosphorylation and nuclear accumulation of T221A was much
        more pronounced in G2/M phase.
- term:
    id: GO:0005840
    label: ribosome
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20217897
  review:
    summary: >-
      Most of the cellular RPS3 pool is located in the ribosome, consistent with
      its core role as a ribosomal protein.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Ribosome localization is core to RPS3's function as a 40S small-subunit
      protein.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20217897
      supporting_text: >-
        Most of the rpS3 pool is located in the ribosome while the minority
        exists in free form in the cytoplasm.
- term:
    id: GO:0019900
    label: kinase binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:20605787
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is a physiological target of Akt kinase; NGF-stimulated Akt
      phosphorylates RPS3 at Thr70, and this phosphorylation is required for Akt
      binding to RPS3.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Kinase binding captures the directly-evidenced Akt interaction that
      regulates RPS3's pro-apoptotic/DNA-repair switch, an extra-ribosomal
      regulatory context. More informative than generic protein binding but
      non-core relative to the ribosomal role.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20605787
      supporting_text: >-
        NGF stimulation resulted in phosphorylation of threonine 70 of RPS3 by
        Akt, and this phosphorylation was required for Akt binding to RPS3.
- term:
    id: GO:0019901
    label: protein kinase binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:21871177
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 interacts with the cell-cycle kinase Cdk1, which phosphorylates it
      during G2/M phase, as shown by co-IP and GST pull-down.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Protein kinase binding captures the directly-evidenced Cdk1 interaction
      that regulates RPS3's mitotic phosphorylation/nuclear accumulation, an
      extra-ribosomal regulatory context. More informative than generic protein
      binding but non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:21871177
      supporting_text: >-
        Co-immunoprecipitation and GST pull-down assays revealed that Cdk1
        interacted with rpS3.
- term:
    id: GO:0042981
    label: regulation of apoptotic process
  evidence_type: IMP
  original_reference_id: PMID:20605787
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 acts as an Akt-regulated molecular switch in neuronal apoptosis;
      abolishing Akt-mediated phosphorylation accelerated apoptotic cell death,
      while RPS3 induced apoptosis via E2F1-dependent gene up-regulation.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Regulation of apoptosis is a context-dependent extra-ribosomal moonlighting
      role of RPS3, secondary to the core ribosomal function. Retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20605787
      supporting_text: >-
        Akt-dependent phosphorylation of RPS3 inhibited its proapoptotic
        function ... Abolishment of Akt-mediated RPS3 phosphorylation ...
        accelerated apoptotic cell death
- term:
    id: GO:0044390
    label: ubiquitin-like protein conjugating enzyme binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:21968017
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 interacts with SUMO-1 (identified by yeast two-hybrid) and is
      sumoylated, which increases its protein stability.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This captures a specific interaction with the SUMO modification machinery
      that regulates RPS3 stability, an extra-ribosomal regulatory context.
      Retained as non-core. [Note: the interactor identified is SUMO-1 itself; the
      "conjugating enzyme binding" framing is a curation interpretation of the
      SUMO-pathway interaction.]
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:21968017
      supporting_text: >-
        we identified small ubiquitin-related modifier-1 (SUMO-1) as a new
        interacting partner of rpS3.
- term:
    id: GO:0005654
    label: nucleoplasm
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-6791223
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 in the nucleoplasm in the context of pre-40S particle
      processing during ribosome biogenesis (pre-rRNA processing en route from
      nucleus to cytosol).
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Nucleoplasmic localization reflects the transient nuclear phase of
      ribosome biogenesis/pre-40S trafficking, not the mature cytosolic
      ribosomal location. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-6791223
      supporting_text: >-
        18SE pre-rRNA in pre-40S particles is nucleolytically processed during
        translocation from the nucleus to the cytosol
- term:
    id: GO:0031397
    label: negative regulation of protein ubiquitination
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19656744
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 interacts with MDM2 and p53; under oxidative stress, RPS3 knockdown
      led to loss of the MDM2 E3 ubiquitin ligase domain and decreased p53,
      implicating RPS3 in regulating MDM2-mediated ubiquitination of p53.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This is an extra-ribosomal moonlighting role in the p53/MDM2 axis. The
      evidence is indirect (effects on MDM2/p53 levels under stress) rather than
      a direct ubiquitination assay, and is secondary to the core ribosomal
      function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19656744
      supporting_text: >-
        in cells exposed to oxidative stress and harboring siRNA to knockdown
        RPS3 expression, decreased p53 levels and loss of the E3 ubiquitin ligase
        domain possessed by MDM2 were observed.
- term:
    id: GO:0032358
    label: oxidized pyrimidine DNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19656744
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 binds an 8-oxoG-containing duplex oligonucleotide and acts as a
      scaffold for binding of MDM2 and p53 at the damaged DNA.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Damaged/oxidized DNA binding by RPS3 is well-supported (PMID:14706345,
      PMID:18610840), and DNA-pull-down here shows binding to an 8-oxoG duplex.
      This is an extra-ribosomal moonlighting activity, secondary to the core
      ribosomal function. [The 8-oxoG lesion is an oxidized purine; the term used
      is oxidized pyrimidine DNA binding, a minor mismatch, but binding to
      oxidatively damaged DNA is supported.]
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19656744
      supporting_text: >-
        DNA pull-down assays using a 7,8-dihydro-8-oxoguanine duplex
        oligonucleotide as a substrate found that RPS3 acted as a scaffold for
        the additional binding of MDM2 and p53
- term:
    id: GO:0003906
    label: DNA-(apurinic or apyrimidinic site) endonuclease activity
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19059439
  review:
    summary: >-
      Phosphorylation of non-ribosomal RPS3 by PKCdelta increased its repair
      (AP/damage) endonuclease activity.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      AP-site endonuclease activity is a historically reported moonlighting
      activity of RPS3. Its intrinsic catalytic relevance in human cells remains
      debated (PMID:18610840 finds only AP-site beta-elimination and questions
      global in vivo repair), so this is retained as a non-core, context-dependent
      activity rather than a core function.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19059439
      supporting_text: >-
        the repair endonuclease activity of rpS3 was increased by its
        phosphorylation.
- term:
    id: GO:0005634
    label: nucleus
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19059439
  review:
    summary: >-
      PKCdelta phosphorylation mobilizes non-ribosomal RPS3 to the nucleus to
      repair damaged DNA.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Stimulus/phosphorylation-dependent nuclear localization is a regulated
      extra-ribosomal context, non-core relative to the cytosolic ribosomal
      location. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19059439
      supporting_text: >-
        PKCdelta phosphorylates rpS3 resulting in its mobilization in the
        nucleus to repair damaged DNA.
- term:
    id: GO:0005737
    label: cytoplasm
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19059439
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is present in the cytoplasm, with a phosphorylation-regulated
      non-ribosomal pool that relocates to the nucleus upon DNA damage.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytoplasm is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3, consistent with
      its cytosolic ribosomal function.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19059439
      supporting_text: >-
        Phosphorylated rpS3 was only detected in non-ribosomal rpS3
- term:
    id: GO:0005840
    label: ribosome
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19059439
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is a component of the 40S ribosomal subunit involved in translation.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Ribosome localization is core to RPS3's role as a small-subunit ribosomal
      protein.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19059439
      supporting_text: >-
        Ribosomal protein S3 (rpS3) is critically involved in translation as a
        component of the 40S ribosomal subunit
- term:
    id: GO:0003677
    label: DNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18610840
  review:
    summary: >-
      Recombinant human RPS3 binds DNA, including undamaged DNA; in this study
      binding to undamaged DNA was actually more efficient than to several
      damaged lesions.
    action: MODIFY
    reason: >-
      Generic DNA binding is less informative than the specific damaged-DNA
      binding activity that is the functionally relevant moonlighting feature of
      RPS3; the same reference also documents damaged DNA binding. Recommend the
      more specific damaged DNA binding term (also separately annotated from this
      reference).
    proposed_replacement_terms:
    - id: GO:0003684
      label: damaged DNA binding
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:18610840
      supporting_text: >-
        Binding of hS3 to DNA ligands containing oxidized and deaminated bases
        was less efficient than its binding to undamaged DNA.
- term:
    id: GO:0003684
    label: damaged DNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18610840
  review:
    summary: >-
      Recombinant human RPS3 binds and cleaves AP-site-containing DNA substrates
      (via beta-elimination), confirming damaged-DNA binding.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Damaged DNA binding is a well-supported extra-ribosomal activity, secondary
      to the core ribosomal function. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:18610840
      supporting_text: >-
        hS3 catalyzed cleavage of apurine/apyrimidine sites through
        beta-elimination mechanism forming a transient Schiff base covalent
        intermediate
- term:
    id: GO:0003906
    label: DNA-(apurinic or apyrimidinic site) endonuclease activity
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18610840
  review:
    summary: >-
      Recombinant human RPS3 cleaves AP sites via a beta-elimination (AP lyase)
      mechanism but does not cleave substrates containing other lesions, and the
      authors question its in vivo global repair relevance.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      AP-site cleavage activity is supported in vitro but is mechanistically an
      AP lyase (beta-elimination) and its physiological/global in vivo relevance
      is doubted by the authors. It is a context-dependent moonlighting activity,
      secondary to the core ribosomal function; retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:18610840
      supporting_text: >-
        the activity of hS3 on apurine/apyrimidine sites is not likely to be
        involved in the global in vivo DNA repair but could have a role in the
        repair in some specific locations in the genome.
- term:
    id: GO:0005634
    label: nucleus
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19460357
  review:
    summary: >-
      Arginine methylation of RPS3 by PRMT1 is required for its import into the
      nucleus/nucleolus and subsequent incorporation into the ribosome.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Nuclear/nucleolar localization here reflects the ribosome-biogenesis
      trafficking phase, not the mature cytosolic ribosomal location. Retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19460357
      supporting_text: >-
        mutant rpS3 (3RA), which cannot be methylated at these sites, cannot be
        transported into the nucleolus and subsequently incorporated into the
        ribosome.
- term:
    id: GO:0005730
    label: nucleolus
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19460357
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is imported into the nucleolus (dependent on PRMT1 arginine
      methylation) for small-subunit ribosome assembly.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Nucleolar localization reflects the ribosome-biogenesis/assembly phase of
      RPS3, distinct from the mature cytosolic ribosomal location. This is a
      direct functional study (unlike the weaker IEA nucleolus annotation that was
      marked over-annotated), so retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19460357
      supporting_text: >-
        arginine methylation of rpS3 plays a critical role in its import into the
        nucleolus, as well as in small subunit assembly of the ribosome.
- term:
    id: GO:0005840
    label: ribosome
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19460357
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is incorporated into the ribosome (40S small subunit), a process that
      depends on its PRMT1-mediated arginine methylation.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Ribosome localization/incorporation is core to RPS3's function as a 40S
      small-subunit ribosomal protein.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19460357
      supporting_text: >-
        The human ribosomal protein S3 (rpS3), a component of the 40S small
        subunit in the ribosome
- term:
    id: GO:0019104
    label: DNA N-glycosylase activity
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18610840
  negated: true
  review:
    summary: >-
      Recombinant human RPS3 cleaved AP sites by beta-elimination but did NOT
      cleave substrates containing oxidized/deaminated bases (8-oxoguanine,
      uracil, hypoxanthine, etc.), so it lacks DNA glycosylase activity.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      The NOT annotation is well-supported: despite sequence-based predictions,
      RPS3 has no detectable DNA N-glycosylase activity. Accept the negation as
      correctly representing the experimental result.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:18610840
      supporting_text: >-
        hS3 catalyzed cleavage of apurine/apyrimidine sites through
        beta-elimination mechanism forming a transient Schiff base covalent
        intermediate, but did not cleave substrates containing other lesions.
- term:
    id: GO:0019899
    label: enzyme binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:19460357
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 is methylated by and interacts with protein arginine
      methyltransferase 1 (PRMT1), which regulates its nucleolar import and
      ribosome assembly.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Enzyme binding here captures the directly-evidenced PRMT1 interaction that
      governs RPS3 modification and ribosome assembly. More informative than
      generic protein binding; it relates to ribosome biogenesis regulation but
      is retained as non-core relative to the structural ribosomal function.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19460357
      supporting_text: >-
        the arginine residue(s) of rpS3 are methylated by protein arginine
        methyltransferase 1 (PRMT1).
- term:
    id: GO:0070181
    label: small ribosomal subunit rRNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:18610840
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 interacts with 18S rRNA, the rRNA of the small (40S) ribosomal
      subunit, consistent with its structural role in the small subunit.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Small-subunit rRNA binding is consistent with RPS3's core structural role
      in the 40S subunit, where it contacts 18S rRNA. The 18S rRNA interaction
      is directly documented (PMID:20217897), supporting this MF as core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20217897
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 protein was found to interact with various RNAs-endogenous rpS3,
        18S rRNA.
- term:
    id: GO:0003906
    label: DNA-(apurinic or apyrimidinic site) endonuclease activity
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:15707971
  review:
    summary: >-
      Mammalian RPS3 nicks DNA containing AP sites and a wide range of
      base-damage lesions (thymine glycols, UV-damaged lesions) as a
      base-damage-endonuclease.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      AP-site endonuclease activity is a historically reported moonlighting
      activity. Its intrinsic catalytic relevance for human RPS3 in vivo is
      debated (PMID:18610840), so it is retained as a context-dependent non-core
      activity rather than a core function.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:15707971
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 cleaves the lesions including AP sites, thymine glycols, and other
        UV damaged lesions such as pyrimidine dimers.
- term:
    id: GO:0004520
    label: DNA endonuclease activity
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:15707971
  review:
    summary: >-
      Mammalian RPS3 acts as a wide-range base-damage endonuclease, cleaving
      phosphodiester bonds in DNA containing various altered bases.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      DNA endonuclease activity is a historically reported moonlighting activity
      whose physiological relevance in human cells is debated. Retained as a
      non-core, context-dependent activity.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:15707971
      supporting_text: >-
        rpS3 cleaves phosphodiester bonds of DNAs containing altered bases with
        broad specificity acting as a base-damage-endonuclease.
- term:
    id: GO:0019104
    label: DNA N-glycosylase activity
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: PMID:15707971
  negated: true
  review:
    summary: >-
      Despite a sequence prediction of glycosylase activity, RPS3 does not have
      glycosylase activity; it acts as an endonuclease on damaged DNA.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      The NOT annotation is well-supported and consistent with PMID:18610840:
      RPS3 lacks DNA glycosylase activity. Accept the negation.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:15707971
      supporting_text: >-
        This enzyme does not have a glycosylase activity as predicted from its
        amino acid sequence.
- term:
    id: GO:0097100
    label: supercoiled DNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:15707971
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 cleaves supercoiled UV-damaged DNA more efficiently than the relaxed
      counterpart, indicating a preference for/interaction with supercoiled DNA.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Supercoiled DNA binding is inferred from the preferential cleavage of
      supercoiled damaged DNA, part of RPS3's debated extra-ribosomal
      base-damage-endonuclease activity. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:15707971
      supporting_text: >-
        RpS3 cleaves supercoiled UV damaged DNA more efficiently than the relaxed
        counterpart
- term:
    id: GO:0003684
    label: damaged DNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:14706345
  review:
    summary: >-
      By surface plasmon resonance, human RPS3 binds 8-oxoG and AP sites with
      very high apparent affinity (orders of magnitude higher than OGG1).
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Damaged DNA binding is among the best-supported extra-ribosomal activities
      of human RPS3, with quantitative binding data. It is secondary to the core
      ribosomal role; retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:14706345
      supporting_text: >-
        An even more dramatic five orders of magnitude higher binding affinity
        for AP DNA was found for hS3 as opposed to hOGG1.
- term:
    id: GO:0032357
    label: oxidized purine DNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:14706345
  review:
    summary: >-
      Human RPS3 binds 7,8-dihydro-8-oxoguanine (8-oxoG), an oxidized purine
      lesion, with high apparent affinity.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Oxidized purine (8-oxoG) DNA binding is directly and quantitatively
      supported; this term is more specific and accurate than the generic
      oxidized-pyrimidine term used elsewhere. It is an extra-ribosomal
      moonlighting activity, secondary to the core ribosomal function. Retained
      as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:14706345
      supporting_text: >-
        hS3 was found to have an apparent three orders of magnitude higher
        binding affinity (KD) for 8-oxoG than the human N-glycosylase/AP lyase
        base excision repair (BER) enzyme OGG1.
- term:
    id: GO:0003906
    label: DNA-(apurinic or apyrimidinic site) endonuclease activity
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:7775413
  review:
    summary: >-
      The earliest report implicating mammalian RPS3 in DNA damage processing
      identified an AP/UV endonuclease activity co-purifying with ribosomal
      protein S3.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      This historical biochemical evidence connects RPS3 with AP/UV endonuclease
      activity, but the physiological relevance for human RPS3 is debated
      (PMID:18610840). Retained as a non-core, context-dependent activity.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:7775413
      supporting_text: >-
        The nuclease activities have been purified from mouse cells to yield a
        peptide of M(r) = 32,000, whose sequence indicates identity with
        ribosomal protein S3.
- term:
    id: GO:0016020
    label: membrane
  evidence_type: HDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:19946888
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 was detected in a high-throughput proteomic study of the membrane
      proteome of NK cells.
    action: MARK_AS_OVER_ANNOTATED
    reason: >-
      Detection of an abundant ribosomal protein in a membrane proteome most
      plausibly reflects co-isolation (e.g., membrane-associated ribosomes) or
      contamination rather than an RPS3-specific integral/peripheral membrane
      role. Treated as an over-annotation.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:19946888
      supporting_text: >-
        Defining the membrane proteome of NK cells.
- term:
    id: GO:0005515
    label: protein binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:24423872
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 directly associates with AUF1 and contributes to 3'UTR-dependent
      translation of Cry1 mRNA in the circadian clock context.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Generic protein binding term; the specific AUF1 association relates to
      translational regulation but is non-core relative to the structural
      ribosomal function and not a defining RPS3 molecular function. Retained as
      non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:24423872
      supporting_text: >-
        AUF1 ... directly associated with ribosomal protein S3 or ribosomal
        protein S14, resulting in translation of Cry1 mRNA in a 3'UTR-dependent
        manner.
- term:
    id: GO:2001235
    label: positive regulation of apoptotic signaling pathway
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:14988002
  review:
    summary: >-
      Overexpression of GFP-RPS3 activates caspase-8/caspase-3 and sensitizes
      cells to cytokine-induced apoptosis, indicating a pro-apoptotic activity.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Pro-apoptotic activity is a context-dependent extra-ribosomal moonlighting
      function shown by overexpression/cytokine paradigms, secondary to the core
      ribosomal role. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:14988002
      supporting_text: >-
        Transient expression of GFP-rpS3 activates caspase-8/caspase-3 and
        sensitizes cytokine-induced apoptosis.
- term:
    id: GO:0003723
    label: RNA binding
  evidence_type: HDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:22658674
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 was identified as an mRNA-binding protein in a high-throughput
      interactome-capture atlas of mammalian mRNA-binding proteins.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      RNA binding is consistent with RPS3's KH domain and its direct rRNA/mRNA
      contacts in the ribosome and in autoregulation (PMID:20217897). It is a real
      activity but, as captured by these broad mRNA-interactome screens, is
      secondary to the core structural ribosomal role; kept non-core for
      consistency with the other RNA binding annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:22658674
      supporting_text: >-
        We identify 860 proteins that qualify as RBPs by biochemical and
        statistical criteria
- term:
    id: GO:0003723
    label: RNA binding
  evidence_type: HDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:22681889
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 was identified among the mRNA-bound proteome in a second
      high-throughput RNA-interactome study.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Independent HDA support for RPS3 RNA binding, consistent with its KH domain
      and rRNA/mRNA contacts. A real but broad MF, secondary to the structural
      ribosomal function; kept non-core for consistency with the other RNA binding
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:22681889
      supporting_text: >-
        We developed a photoreactive nucleotide-enhanced UV crosslinking and
        oligo(dT) purification approach to identify the mRNA-bound proteome
- term:
    id: GO:0003684
    label: damaged DNA binding
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:17560175
  review:
    summary: >-
      Human RPS3 has affinity for abasic sites and 8-oxoG residues and
      co-localizes with 8-oxoG damage foci after genotoxic stress.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Damaged DNA binding is a well-supported extra-ribosomal activity here
      complemented by in-cell co-localization with damage foci. Secondary to the
      core ribosomal role; retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:17560175
      supporting_text: >-
        hS3 was traced in exposed cells to its co-localization with 8-oxoG foci
- term:
    id: GO:0005634
    label: nucleus
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:16814409
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 was observed in the nucleus as well as cytoplasm/cell periphery; in
      this study it colocalized with Nm23-H1 in the cell periphery and cytoplasm.
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Nuclear localization is a regulated extra-ribosomal pool, non-core relative
      to the cytosolic ribosomal location. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:16814409
      supporting_text: >-
        RpS3 interacted with nm23-H1, and the two proteins were colocalized in
        the cell periphery and cytoplasm.
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: IDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:17560175
  review:
    summary: >-
      Under basal conditions RPS3 is cytoplasmic/cytosolic, translocating to the
      nucleus only upon genotoxic stress.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3's cytosolic
      ribosomal function, consistent with accepted cytosol annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:17560175
      supporting_text: >-
        hS3 is capable of carrying out both roles by its ex vivo translocation
        from the cytoplasm to the nucleus as a consequence of genotoxic stress.
- term:
    id: GO:0019899
    label: enzyme binding
  evidence_type: IPI
  original_reference_id: PMID:15518571
  review:
    summary: >-
      Human RPS3 physically interacts with the BER enzymes OGG1 and APE1/Ref-1
      (co-immunoprecipitation and SPR).
    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    reason: >-
      Enzyme binding captures the directly-evidenced interactions with BER
      enzymes (OGG1, APE1), more informative than generic protein binding but
      part of RPS3's extra-ribosomal BER-cofactor role. Retained as non-core.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:15518571
      supporting_text: >-
        Human S3 was found to co-immunoprecipitate with both hOGG1 and
        APE/Ref-1, indicating that these proteins physically interact with one
        another.
- term:
    id: GO:0070062
    label: extracellular exosome
  evidence_type: HDA
  original_reference_id: PMID:20458337
  review:
    summary: >-
      RPS3 was detected in a proteomic study of MHC class II-associated proteins
      in B-cell exosomes.
    action: MARK_AS_OVER_ANNOTATED
    reason: >-
      Detection of an abundant ribosomal protein in an exosome proteome is a
      common, non-specific finding and does not establish a discrete
      RPS3-specific exosomal function. Treated as an over-annotation.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: PMID:20458337
      supporting_text: >-
        MHC class II-associated proteins in B-cell exosomes and potential
        functional implications for exosome biogenesis.
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-156808
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-156808
      supporting_text: >-
        Formation of translation initiation complexes yielding circularized Ceruloplasmin mRNA in a  'closed-loop' conformation
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-156823
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-156823
      supporting_text: >-
        Association of phospho-L13a with GAIT element of Ceruloplasmin mRNA
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-157849
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-157849
      supporting_text: >-
        Formation of translation initiation complexes containing mRNA that does not circularize
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-1799332
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-1799332
      supporting_text: >-
        Nascent polypeptide:mRNA:ribosome complex binds signal recognition particle (SRP)
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-1799335
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-1799335
      supporting_text: >-
        Synthesis of nascent polypeptide containing signal sequence
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-192704
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-192704
      supporting_text: >-
        Synthesis of PB1-F2
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-192841
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-192841
      supporting_text: >-
        Viral Protein Synthesis
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-6791223
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a pre-40S/40S ribosome component) in the cytosol
      in the context of pre-rRNA processing during nucleus-to-cytosol
      translocation of pre-40S particles.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome, consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-6791223
      supporting_text: >-
        18SE pre-rRNA in pre-40S particles is nucleolytically processed during
        translocation from the nucleus to the cytosol
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72619
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72619
      supporting_text: >-
        eIF2:GTP is hydrolyzed, eIFs are released
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72621
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72621
      supporting_text: >-
        Ribosomal scanning
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72671
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72671
      supporting_text: >-
        eIF5B:GTP is hydrolyzed and released
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72672
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72672
      supporting_text: >-
        The 60S subunit joins the translation initiation complex
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72673
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-72673
      supporting_text: >-
        Release of 40S and 60S subunits from the 80S ribosome

    id: GO:0005829

- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol

- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-9948300
  review:

      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
      cytosolic ribosome participating in translation and ribosome-associated
      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-9948300
      supporting_text: >-
        RQT complex:K63polyUb-80S ribosome dissociates yielding K63polyUb-40S subunit and 60S subunit:peptidyl-tRNA with nascent peptide
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-9948301
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the

      processes. Consistent with the accepted cytosol/cytosolic ribosome
      annotations.
    supported_by:
    - reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-9948301
      supporting_text: >-
        ZNF598 K63 polyubiquitinates RPS10 (eS10) and RPS20 (uS10) of the 40S ribosomal subunit
- term:
    id: GO:0005829
    label: cytosol
  evidence_type: TAS
  original_reference_id: Reactome:R-HSA-9948317
  review:
    summary: >-
      Reactome places RPS3 (as a 40S/cytosolic ribosome component) in the cytosol
      within this translation/ribosome-associated pathway step.
    action: ACCEPT
    reason: >-
      Cytosol is the expected, core cellular location for RPS3 as part of the
